edges.description: CLN3 disease late endosome/lysosome fractions accumulate cholesterol, supporting a cholesterol storage branch of the disease mechanism.
CLN3 endolysosomal dysfunction causes rapidly progressive retinal degeneration and visual failure.
CLN3 disease progresses from cognitive and behavioral dysfunction to seizures, motor decline, and later severe neuropsychiatric/autonomic manifestations.

nodes.description: CLN3 dysfunction perturbs endolysosomal membrane biology, lysosomal cargo handling, and cholesterol trafficking. The resulting lysosomal storage phenotype includes fingerprint inclusions in patient material and cholesterol accumulation in late endosome/lysosome fractions, contributing to progressive retinal and neuronal degeneration.

evidence.snippet: Blood film microscopy revealed vacuolated lymphocytes, and electron microscopy showed lysosomal (fingerprint) inclusions in all 8 patients.
INTERPRETATION: Our results support that JNCL is a lysosomal cholesterol storage disorder.

nodes.description: CLN3 disease late endosome/lysosome fractions accumulate cholesterol, supporting a lysosomal cholesterol storage mechanism that overlaps with other lipid-trafficking lysosomal disorders.

evidence.snippet: INTERPRETATION: Our results support that JNCL is a lysosomal cholesterol storage disorder.

nodes.description: CLN3 disease progresses to cognitive impairment, behavioral symptoms, seizures, motor deterioration, sleep disruption, and advanced adolescent or adult neuropsychiatric/autonomic episodes.

evidence.snippet: behavioral and cognitive dysfunction at 7 to 10 years of age, and progressive motor decline and seizures at 10 to 13 years of age
Recurrent non-epileptic episodes of frightened facial and body expression occur in more than half of post-adolescent patients with juvenile neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis (JNCL, CLN3 disease).

nodes.description: CLN3 disease causes early, rapidly progressive retinal degeneration with macular atrophy, outer retinal loss, electrophysiologic abnormalities, and visual failure.

evidence.snippet: Participants presented with rapid bilateral vision loss over 1 to 18 months
